title: MCP Structured Error
description: A standardized error response format that enables clients to react intelligently — retry, inform the user, or try an alternative approach.

message.description: Human-readable error description. Should be specific enough to be useful, not generic.

category.description: Error classification that drives client behavior: 'transient' = retry may succeed; 'permanent' = do not retry; 'auth_required' = re-authenticate; 'invalid_input' = fix parameters; 'rate_limited' = wait and retry.

retry_after_seconds.description: Seconds to wait before retrying. Only meaningful for 'transient' and 'rate_limited' categories.

user_actionable.description: Whether this error should be surfaced to the user. If false, the client should handle it silently (e.g., automatic retry).

suggestion.description: A concrete next step the client or user can take to resolve the issue.
examples: Narrow the date range to reduce query cost.
Request the 'write:issues' scope to perform this operation.
The resource was moved — use ID 'PROJ-456' instead.

details.description: Additional structured context. Schema varies by error code.

documentation_url.description: Link to documentation explaining this error in detail.
